_risk_level_doc: Change to a security hook. Medium because the fix is additive (moving an existing block earlier + switching one check to use a pre-existing stripped view) and the fail-closed invariant (on stripping failure, raw command is used) is preserved.

requirement.original: Fix a false positive in the bash safety hook: when codex exec --prompt "..." or graphify-query.py --requirement "..." is invoked and the prompt/requirement TEXT happens to contain the name of a protected script like write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py, the hook blocks the command as if the script were being executed.
requirement.clarified: Layer 1.F entry gate in hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh uses raw $COMMAND substring matching, which fires when the protected name appears inside a quoted string argument to an unrelated command. Fix by making the entry gate use the context-stripped command view (COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED), which strips quoted string argument content for non-interpreter, non-danger commands.
requirement.what: Change Layer 1.F entry gate to use COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED (produced by bash_context_strip.py) instead of raw $COMMAND, by moving the context-strip block to run before Layer 1.F.
requirement.why: Commands like 'codex exec --prompt "...write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py..."' are legitimate tool invocations that mention the protected script name only in text-argument context. The current substring gate treats argument text as executable context, causing false positives.

included: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh — Layer 1.F entry gate + context-strip block relocation
excluded: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py — stripping logic is already correct, no changes needed
Any non-hook scripts or agent files

success_criteria: Compound commands containing write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py still exit 2 (BLOCK)
All existing tests in test_bash_safety_context_rules.py pass
New TestBulkSentinelFalsePositive test class with >= 2 tests passes
constraints: Do not modify any hook other than pretool-bash-safety.sh
Fail-closed invariant: if context stripping fails, COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED = raw $COMMAND

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths. PreToolUse Bash safety hook, server-side only.

evidence.observed: graphify-query.py --requirement "Add positive regression test for CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback path in write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py" — BLOCKED. codex exec --prompt "...write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py..." — BLOCKED. Additionally confirmed during BA self-review: PROMPT='...write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py...' shell variable assignment in a compound Bash command was itself blocked by Layer 1.F.

measured.value: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 669-670 use 'grep -qF write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' against raw $COMMAND; the context-strip block that would neutralise this is at lines 1091-1104 (after Layer 1.F).

expected.value: Layer 1.F entry gate should match only when the protected name appears in an executable context, not in a quoted string argument to an unrelated command.
expected.source: User requirement document + bash_context_strip.py which already implements this classification

evidence.gap: Context stripping runs ~420 lines after Layer 1.F. Switching the entry gate to COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED eliminates the false positive while preserving all real-execution blocking via the stripping library's fail-closed fallback and interpreter-aware exposure.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Commands with quoted string arguments containing 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' are blocked by Layer 1.F even though the script name is not being executed.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Layer 1.F entry gate at line 669-670 uses grep -qF substring match on raw $COMMAND. Raw $COMMAND includes verbatim quoted argument text. The context-stripping facility that strips quoted non-executable argument content is initialised at line 1091, after Layer 1.F.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — design decision in initial Layer 1.F implementation (tasks 20260524-205206 + 20260525-095242 + 20260526-053746)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Layer 1.F was added to prevent sentinel writes and was placed early in the hook for fast-fail. The context-stripping block was added to the hook later (dev-20260529-092512) to address a different class of false positives (dd/mkfs/kill rules). The two were never integrated.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Any bash command mentioning the protected name in any string context — including tool invocations with long prompt arguments — is incorrectly blocked.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Layer 1.F entry gate predates the context-strip block. The gap between them was always present but only became problematic when commands with long prompt/requirement arguments referencing the protected script were attempted.
observations: grep -qF 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' on raw $COMMAND confirmed at line 669-670 (Read tool, this session)
COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED initialised at line 1091-1104 (Read tool, this session)
bash_context_strip.py _process_segment generic path strips double-quoted args to '' for non-interpreter commands (Read tool lines 316-330, this session)
Live false positive reproduced: BA's own Bash call with PROMPT='...' containing the protected name was blocked by Layer 1.F (observed during self-review)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete (observed false positive live; source code verified)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Live reproduction: Bash call with PROMPT variable containing script name was blocked with stderr message 'BLOCKED: bulk-commit-sentinel-write'. Source read confirmed grep -qF on raw $COMMAND at lines 669-670.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — not a regression; this is a design gap between two features added at different times

pre_existing_guards.purpose: M5 canonical venv-activate form allowlist — checked against raw $COMMAND before Python tokenizer. Must remain on raw $COMMAND.
Fail-closed: only overwrite COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED on exit 0 from stripping helper. On failure, raw command is used.

development_approach.strategy: Move the context-strip block (lines 1091-1104) verbatim to just before Layer 1.F (line 669). Change the Layer 1.F entry gate's two grep -qF calls from $COMMAND to $COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED. Keep all inner Layer 1.F logic (M5 check, Python tokenizer, BARE_WRITER/PURE_READ/DENY branching) on raw $COMMAND — only the entry gate changes.
development_approach.validation_approach: Run pytest on test_bash_safety_context_rules.py; manually verify the two reported false-positive commands exit 0 by invoking the hook script directly with piped JSON payloads.

Hook invocation with command: python3 -c "import os; os.system('python3 scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py')"
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 2 (BLOCK) — python3 -c exposes payload via _process_shell_interp

acceptance_criteria.given: Hook invocation with compound command: echo test && python3 scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py --output-dir /tmp

acceptance_criteria.then: All tests pass including the new AC1 and AC2 regression tests

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex invocation was itself blocked by Layer 1.F (the script name in the PROMPT= variable assignment triggered the entry gate) — a live demonstration of the exact bug. Self-review substituted.

findings.issue: Codex Bash call blocked by the hook under analysis — the protected script name in PROMPT='...' variable assignment triggered Layer 1.F entry gate
findings.rationale: This is not a codex-generated finding; it is a live reproduction of the false positive expanding to variable assignment contexts. Included as observed evidence.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted; adversarial questions self-answered in Root Cause Deep-Dive section (python3 -c payload exposure, bash -c nesting, variable assignment handling, unterminated-quote fail-closed)
